answer: The capital city of Canada is Ottawa. It is located in Ontario and serves as the political center of the country.

results.title: The 13 Capital Cities of Canada - ThoughtCo
results.snippet: The capital of Canada is Ottawa, which was incorporated in 1855 and gets its name from the Algonquin word for trade.

results.title: Capital City of Canada: Why Ottawa? · PrepScholar
results.snippet: # Capital City of Canada: Why Ottawa? In this guide, **we'll answer an important question everyone should know the answer to: what is the capital of Canada?**In addition, we'll explain how this place came to be the capital city of Canada and what all the capital cities of the Canadian provinces and territories are currently. The next 16 years witnessed ongoing disputes over what the capital of the new province should be; contenders included Quebec City, Toronto, Montreal, Kingston, and finally Ottawa. It wasn’t until 1866 that Ottawa was officially designated the capital city of the Province of Canada and began to hold Parliament. We now know that Ottawa is the capital city of Canada, but what about the capital cities of all the provinces and territories within Canada? The following chart depicts the capital city of each Canadian province/territory and its population from the 2016 census. For most provinces/territories in Canada, the capital city is also the most populated city, but this isn’t always the case.

results.title: Canada's Capitals - Canadian Capital Cities Organization
results.snippet: # Canada’s Capitals. Canada’s capitals are alive with stories that emphasize our history, our heritage and our cultures. Fredericton is the capital of the province of New Brunswick and is home to over 56,224 residents. Iqaluit is Nunavut’s capital and Canada’s coolest arctic city! Ottawa, Canada’s National Capital is situated at the confluence of the majestic Rideau, Gatineau and des Outaouais, and is home to over 900,000 residents — making it the fourth-largest city in Canada. Quebec City is the capital of the Province of Quebec and is home to over 516,000 people. Established as a City in 1834, Toronto became the capital of Ontario when the Province was created at Confederation in 1867. Established as the Yukon's capital city in 1953, the City of Whitehorse is home to some of the most spectacular scenery in Canada. Yellowknife is a culturally rich capital thriving with diversity, and home to about 20,000 people.

results.snippet: Ottawa is Canada’s capital city and has long been known as an important hub for commerce and travel. ## Where is Ottawa? ## History of Ottawa. ## What to do in Ottawa. ottawa river near parliament hill in canada. rideau canal view in ottawa city in summer. canadian war museum in ottawa city. Ottawa has a history of being a paranormal city. Visit Ottawa’s supposedly most haunted buildings, like the Carleton County Jail, where the last execution in Canada took place. ## Festivals in Ottawa. Ottawa hosts some of the best world-class festivals focused on Canadian arts, history, and culture. Winterlude is a winter celebration festival held in February and is jointly hosted by Ottawa and Gatineau. winterlude festival in ottawa city in canada. ### Ottawa Fringe Festival. Each June since 1997, Ottawa hosts their Fringe Festival, an independent event celebrating local arts and culture. Ottawa’s main station hosts Via Rail, giving easy access to other Canadian cities by train.
